mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held above 5% for multiple consecutive months raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as higher-for-longer policy erodes risk appetite.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.12 in latest snapshot, highest since 2007
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX posts positive median 60d returns during periods FEDFUNDS > 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection from labor-market tightness to slack, historically preceding earnings downgrades and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5 near multi-decade lows as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: In episodes where UNRATE rises >0.3pp in 3 months, SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 5% while CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth decelerates, the joint pattern signals a disinflationary soft landing where markets price forthcoming rate cuts without a growth shock, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change decelerating for 3+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 5.12 and CPIAUCSL 304.348 as of latest snapshot indicating restrictive but stabilizing policy
failure_condition: During joint high-rate/decelerating-inflation episodes, SPX 20d forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, sustained month-over-month declines in HOUST reflect rate-sensitive housing demand contraction that leads broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as the housing channel transmits into consumer wealth and construction employment.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1434 thousand units, below pre-2022 tightening levels
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns following 3+ month HOUST declines are positive in over 55% of tightening-cycle instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows restricted to tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in INDPRO over the trailing 3 months signals expanding industrial output and corporate revenue momentum, which is positively priced into equities over the following week as short-horizon momentum traders extrapolate improving fundamentals.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.2468 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing INDPRO trend and forward 5d SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS month-over-month gains decelerate below a threshold consistent with historical pre-recession slowdowns (e.g., sub-100k average over 3 months), it signals weakening labor demand that leads earnings deceleration and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100000
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156342 thousand persons in latest snapshot
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns following sub-100k PAYEMS 3-month average episodes are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with a historically low unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly indicate resilient consumer demand supported by strong labor income, sustaining corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; the effect requires both conditions jointly since strong sales alone can reflect inflation pass-through while low unemployment alone can precede late-cycle overheating.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 696354 million dollars and UNRATE at 3.5 in latest snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: During joint high-RSAFS-growth/low-UNRATE episodes, SPX 60d forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term fails to outperform either single-proxy model in out-of-sample Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of restrictive policy (FEDFUNDS above 5%), a month-over-month deceleration in CPIAUCSL is interpreted by markets as increasing the probability of near-term rate cuts, producing an immediate positive short-horizon S&P 500 reaction within the following week; this mechanism is absent or reversed when policy is accommodative since disinflation carries different signaling content.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 304.348 and FEDFUNDS at 5.12 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy regime with disinflation
failure_condition: SPX 5d forward returns show no significant positive relationship (t-test p > 0.05) with month-over-month CPIAUCSL deceleration during periods when FEDFUNDS > 5%, or the effect reverses sign in accommodative regimes without being flagged as regime-conditional.
